expectations.text: Identifies the origin as the architecture document / compose stage rather than the spec or the upstream facts
expectations.evidence: "For a typical 'the decomposition is wrong, slices overlap' finding where the underlying facts still hold, the trace lands on the architecture document."

expectations.text: Re-enters the compose stage in a correction/revision mode that revises the existing document, rather than regenerating from scratch
expectations.evidence: Step 4: correction mode 'reads the prior architecture output rather than starting blank', 're-derives only what the correction touches', 'keeps the rest where it's still correct'.

expectations.text: Carries the correction to the stage as a declared, auditable input rather than as undeclared prompt context or an ad-hoc note
expectations.evidence: Step 2 declares a dedicated input with round/origin=validating-stage/target/change/provenance=finding id; 'never as a quiet edit'.

expectations.text: Does NOT edit or modify the spec to resolve the overlapping-slices problem
expectations.evidence: "the architecture correction flow never edits the spec to make an architecture problem disappear."

expectations.text: Does NOT treat owner escalation or a pause as required for this architecture-document-origin correction
expectations.evidence: "Bring in the owner only if the trace lands on the spec"; pause described as off by default and optional.

expectations.text: Treats the three-failure cap as a signal that the root cause is NOT in the plan/target being retried, and widens the search to trace outward (architecture, upstream facts, spec, or elsewhere)
expectations.evidence: "reaching the cap is ... the evidence that the problem probably isn't in that target"; 'the search widens and traces outward'.

expectations.text: Does NOT simply stop and hand the unsolved problem to the owner to diagnose
expectations.evidence: "it is not a reason to dump it on you"; "for most outcomes ... the agents resolve it at the source themselves and you don't have to do anything."

expectations.text: Routes to deeper root-cause investigation that is distinct from the reviewer, rather than letting the reviewer keep driving retries
expectations.evidence: "hands off automatically to the external investigator ... deliberately a different job than the design review that's been bouncing the plan".

expectations.text: Does NOT reflexively rewrite or blame the spec just because the plan failed multiple times
expectations.evidence: "Don't reflexively rewrite the spec ... There is no 'failed N times, so edit the spec' rule."

expectations.text: Keeps the owner uninvolved unless and until the trace concludes the origin is the spec
expectations.evidence: "You are brought in only if the trace concludes the origin is the spec."

expectations.text: Source-traces the missing component to the spec itself as the origin (a spec gap / drift), not merely an architecture mistake
expectations.evidence: "Your phrasing ... leans hard toward the second case ... That makes the origin the spec."

expectations.text: Does NOT edit or patch the spec in-flow as a workaround to keep going
expectations.evidence: "The spec may be changed on that basis, but never in-flow".

expectations.text: Does NOT route the spec change through /foundation (which is for spec creation only)
expectations.evidence: "never through /foundation (which only creates specs)".

expectations.text: Involves/escalates to the owner specifically because the origin is the spec
expectations.evidence: Step 7 'Bring in the owner â€” for the spec-origin outcome ... escalate to the owner.'

expectations.text: Routes the change out to the external spec-modification path rather than handling it silently inside the architecture flow
expectations.evidence: "It routes out to the external spec-modification path, and this is the destination that brings in the owner."

expectations.text: Identifies the origin as the architecture document / compose stage rather than the spec or the upstream facts
expectations.evidence: Names the classification audit (an upstream stage) as 'the most likely real culprit' and its decision rule re-enters at classification audit, i.e. routes upstream rather than to the architecture document.

expectations.text: Re-enters the compose stage in a correction/revision mode that revises the existing document, rather than regenerating from scratch
expectations.evidence: Frames the fix as 're-run forward through every later stage' and 're-compose at the level'; treats it as a directed re-run of the stage, not a correction-mode that revises the existing document preserving correct material.

expectations.text: Carries the correction to the stage as a declared, auditable input rather than as undeclared prompt context or an ad-hoc note
expectations.evidence: Section 5 'Feed the finding back in as input ... as an explicit constraint' and Section 7 'Record the correction' with provenance.

expectations.text: Does NOT edit or modify the spec to resolve the overlapping-slices problem
expectations.evidence: No spec edit proposed; the fix stays in classification/compose. Spec mentioned only as a later fallback to check, not edited.

expectations.text: Does NOT treat owner escalation or a pause as required for this architecture-document-origin correction
expectations.evidence: No owner escalation or pause is invoked for this case.

expectations.text: Treats the three-failure cap as a signal that the root cause is NOT in the plan/target being retried, and widens the search to trace outward (architecture, upstream facts, spec, or elsewhere)
expectations.evidence: "Three consecutive failures ... is a signal, not a setback ... one of the inputs the plan rests on is wrong"; escalates the fix up the pipeline (architecture/spec).

expectations.text: Does NOT simply stop and hand the unsolved problem to the owner to diagnose
expectations.evidence: Step 4 'Bring the human in. This is the right moment' and presents the owner a decision menu (fix upstream / split / re-scope / waive / cut) â€” i.e. hands the decision to the owner.

expectations.text: Routes to deeper root-cause investigation that is distinct from the reviewer, rather than letting the reviewer keep driving retries
expectations.evidence: Diagnoses inline then escalates to the human for the decision; no investigation function distinct from review is invoked.

expectations.text: Does NOT reflexively rewrite or blame the spec just because the plan failed multiple times
expectations.evidence: Branch A checks architecture first and only amends the spec 'if internally contradictory'; not a reflexive rewrite.

expectations.text: Keeps the owner uninvolved unless and until the trace concludes the origin is the spec
expectations.evidence: Owner is brought in at the cap for every branch (split, re-scope, waive, cut), not only for a spec-origin outcome.

expectations.text: Source-traces the missing component to the spec itself as the origin (a spec gap / drift), not merely an architecture mistake
expectations.evidence: "This is not 'the architecture has a bug.' ... The spec is missing a requirement ... This is the root defect."

expectations.text: Does NOT edit or patch the spec in-flow as a workaround to keep going
expectations.evidence: Step 1 'Amend the spec ... Reopen the spec and add the requirement' â€” directly edits the spec inside the correction flow, which CL-012 forbids.

expectations.text: Does NOT route the spec change through /foundation (which is for spec creation only)
expectations.evidence: Sends only a 'root-cause note to foundation' so the gap doesn't recur; the change itself is made by directly amending the spec, not routed through /foundation.

expectations.text: Involves/escalates to the owner specifically because the origin is the spec
expectations.evidence: Step 0 'Who is the authority ... needs the spec owner / requester to confirm it, not an inference made mid-build.'

expectations.text: Routes the change out to the external spec-modification path rather than handling it silently inside the architecture flow
expectations.evidence: Handles the spec change by amending the spec directly in-flow; no external spec-modification path is used.
